summary: A high‑energy Alpine Valley show – highlighted by the first ever performance of “Foolish Heart” and strong jams – recorded on a modest audience tape that’s cloudy but still conveys the vibe.
review: The concert is remembered for its scorching summer heat, relentless crowd enthusiasm and standout moments such as the debut of “Foolish Heart”, ripping renditions of “Mississippi”, “Bird Song” and a monster second‑set sequence (GDTRFB → Miracle → Fantasy → Throwing → NFA).  The recording is an audience capture that suffers from limited clarity and a ‘cloudy’ mix, yet it preserves the feeling of the night for fans who value atmosphere over fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance (average 4‑5 stars) while several note poor audio quality; weighting musical strength (70 %) against recording issues (30 %) yields a solid ~4.1 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cloudy, low‑fidelity mix with limited clarity; some reviewers call it “B” quality or “lacking in quality,” but the tape still captures crowd atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Smooth segues (e.g., Mississippi into Stranger) and a powerful second set that kept the energy high; reviewers note the band let jams breathe and the rhythm section provided solid “meat” throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fiery guitar work, strong vocal delivery and turning up the heat in the latter part of the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for adding extra heat in the second set and delivering solid licks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s confidence on keyboards (e.g., “Never Trust”) is noted as a high point.
band_member_comments.Drums: The rhythm section/drums are described as providing the meat of the performance, with a dark, driving feel.
